title: Standing Up for Everyone's Rights
story: December 10 marks Human Rights Day, when people around the world celebrate fairness and equality for everyone. Schools hold special assemblies about treating others with respect. Communities organize marches and events to remind everyone that all people deserve kindness, safety, and freedom no matter who they are.
activity_question: What do schools often do on Human Rights Day?
activity_choices: Hold special assemblies about treating others with respect
Cancel all classes for the day
Have pizza parties in every classroom
activity_answer: Hold special assemblies about treating others with respect
follow_up_question: What's one way you can stand up for someone's rights at school?

title: Dewey's Decimal Day of Organization
story: Libraries everywhere celebrate their secret weapon on December 10 — the Dewey Decimal System! This clever numbering trick helps librarians organize millions of books so you can find exactly what you need. Every book gets a special number based on its topic, making library adventures way easier and faster.
activity_question: What does the Dewey Decimal System help organize?
follow_up_question: What would happen if libraries didn't have any organization system at all?

title: Paws for a Cause Day
story: Every December 10, people around the world celebrate International Animal Rights Day by standing up for creatures who can't speak for themselves. Activists organize peaceful marches, share photos of rescued animals on social media, and educate others about treating all animals with kindness and respect. Many families visit animal shelters to volunteer, donate supplies, or even adopt a furry friend. Schools often host special assemblies where kids learn about protecting wildlife and farm animals. Some people choose to eat plant-based meals for the day to show support for animal welfare, while others write letters to lawmakers asking for stronger animal protection laws.
activity_question: What do many families do on International Animal Rights Day to help animals directly?
activity_choices: Visit animal shelters to volunteer or adopt
Teach their pets to march in parades
Host costume parties for their dogs
activity_answer: Visit animal shelters to volunteer or adopt
follow_up_question: What's one simple way you could help animals in your own neighborhood?

title: Wyoming Day: Big Sky State Pride
story: Wyoming shows off its state pride every December 10 on Wyoming Day! People across the cowboy state celebrate by sharing photos of beautiful mountains, vast plains, and famous landmarks like Yellowstone. Schools teach students about Wyoming's history, while families enjoy local foods and traditions that make their state special.
activity_question: What do people share on social media during Wyoming Day?
activity_choices: Pictures of their pets in cowboy hats
Recipes for pancakes shaped like Wyoming
bonus_fact: Wyoming was the first state to give women the right to vote!
